evals.expected_output: A complete Swift Testing migration using struct, init, @Test, #expect, #require, and proper error testing patterns

evals.prompt: I'm writing tests for an async notification system. I need to verify that when a user places an order, exactly 3 notifications are sent (one to the customer, one to the kitchen, and one to the delivery system), and that no error notifications are sent. How do I write this with Swift Testing?
evals.expected_output: Test using confirmation() with expectedCount: 3 for the success notifications and a separate confirmation with expectedCount: 0 for error notifications

evals.prompt:
I need to write a parameterized test that validates our URL parser against a bunch of different inputs. Some should succeed and some should fail. Here are my test cases:
- "https://example.com" → valid, host is "example.com"
- "ftp://files.example.com/doc.pdf" → valid, host is "files.example.com"
- "not a url" → should throw InvalidURL
- "" → should throw InvalidURL
- "https://" → should throw MissingHost

I'm on Swift 6 with Xcode 16. How should I structure this?
evals.expected_output: A parameterized test using @Test(arguments:) with a collection of test cases, using an enum or struct for the test data that handles both success and failure cases
